sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Level 2 has a different layout. Let me understand it:
- Left panel (dark gray, cols 1-30): has a small dark object and buttons
- Right panel (white, cols 32-61): has a checkerboard with a yellow L-shape and buttons
- Bottom: two colored boxes (blue and gray) and a blue blob trigger
- The yellow object is at rows 7-11, cols 44-49 (L-shape, 4x4)
- There's another yellow object at rows 24-27, cols 45-48 (smaller, 3x4)
- Buttons are in two sets: left panel (cols 7-24) and right panel (cols 38-55)
- Each panel has 4 horizontal buttons (row 33) and 4 vertical buttons (rows 35-49)
- The blue blob is at cols 42-50, rows 54-62
Let me look at the left panel more carefully. It has a dark gray background with a small dark object at rows 16-19, cols 22-25. The buttons on the left are all black (3-pixel bars at row 33) and gray (3-pixel bars at rows 35-49).
The right panel has a checkerboard with:
- Yellow L-shape at rows 7-11, cols 44-49
- Yellow square at rows 24-27, cols 45-48
- Buttons at rows 33 (horizontal) and rows 35-49 (vertical)
The two colored boxes at the bottom:
- Blue box: rows 54-62, cols 7-15 (left side)
- Blue blob: rows 54-62, cols 42-50 (right side)
Wait, I need to re-examine. Let me look at the ASCII to understand the layout better.

print("\n\nRight panel top (rows 3-15, cols 32-61):")
for i in range(3, 16):
    print(f"Row {i}: {lines[i][32:62]}")

print("\n\nBottom area (rows 50-63):")
for i in range(50, 64):
    print(f"Row {i}: {lines[i][1:62]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me understand the button layout for Level 2
# Left panel buttons (cols 7-24):
# - H bars at row 33: cols 7-9, 12-14, 17-19, 22-24 (currently black)
# - V bars at rows 35-37, 41-43, 47-49: cols 8, 13, 18, 23 (currently gray)
# Right panel buttons (cols 38-55):
# - H bars at row 33: cols 38-40, 43-45, 48-50, 53-55 (currently gray)
# - V bars at rows 35-37, 41-43, 47-49: cols 39, 44, 49, 54 (currently gray)

# Let me first try clicking the blue blob to see the default animation
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 46}])

if last_animation:
    print(f"Animation: {len(last_animation)} entries")
    for i, entry in enumerate(last_animation):
        print(f"  Action {i}: {len(entry.frames)} frames")
        for j, frame in enumerate(entry.frames):
            seg = frame.segmentation
            y_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y' and min(r for r,c in n['boundary']) < 30]
            if y_nodes:
                n = y_nodes[0]
                print(f"    Frame {j}: top-left=({n['boundary'][0][0]}, {n['boundary'][0][1]}), pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

# Let me understand the button layout for Level 2

action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 46}])

if last_animation:
    print(f"Animation: {len(last_animation)} entries")
    for i, entry in enumerate(last_animation):
        print(f"  Action {i}: {len(entry.frames)} frames")
        for j, frame in enumerate(entry.frames):
            seg = frame.segmentation
            y_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y' and min(r for r,c in n['boundary']) < 30]
            if y_nodes:
                n = y_nodes[0]
                print(f"    Frame {j}: top-left=({n['boundary'][0][0]}, {n['boundary'][0][1]}), pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
